paper_title: Improved approximation algorithms for hitting 3-vertex paths
summary: The conjecture that Cluster-VD admits a 2-approximation in polynomial time was resolved affirmatively by Aprile, Drescher, Fiorini, and Huynh in 2020. Their paper arXiv:2007.08057 presents the first 2-approximation algorithm for the (weighted) cluster vertex deletion problem, matching the UGC-hardness lower bound of 2 established via a reduction from Vertex Cover. The result was published in Mathematical Programming in 2021.

since_posted.title: A Tight Approximation Algorithm for the Cluster Vertex Deletion Problem
since_posted.authors: Manuel Aprile, Matthew Drescher, Samuel Fiorini, Tony Huynh
since_posted.claim: Proves the first 2-approximation algorithm for weighted Cluster Vertex Deletion, establishing that the approximation ratio is tight under the Unique Games Conjecture.

search_queries: Cluster Vertex Deletion 2-approximation algorithm polynomial time
Fiorini Joret Schaudt "cluster vertex deletion" approximation 2019 arXiv 1808.10370
notes: The conjecture was posed in the source paper (arXiv:1808.10370) and resolved the same year as the journal publication of the source paper. Two of the four authors of the resolving paper (Fiorini and Huynh) are connected to the graph-theory community tracked in the curated corpus. No internal corpus references to this conjecture were supplied.
